entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The ongoing discussion regarding inflation in the context of on-chain services highlights the potential utility these services could have as an incentive mechanism to manage transaction fees. Particularly, the introduction of simple on-chain "payment codes" is seen as a promising approach that, if implemented judiciously, would allow Bitcoin operators to balance the network's utility growth with the economic impacts of halvings. This careful management is crucial to maintaining a sustainable ecosystem where enhancements in utility do not adversely affect the network's fee structure.
Moreover, it is observed that the Lightning Network might be impacting the fees generated from on-chain transactions. This shift suggests a need for future protocol developments to incorporate features that address both data compression and privacy concerns without compromising the underlying economic model of the blockchain. Protocols like MWEB (Mimblewimble Extension Blocks) are mentioned as examples that could potentially align with these needs by offering solutions that consider the importance of keeping transaction fees within a reasonable range while enhancing the privacy and efficiency of transactions.
